formulas.name: 1. Molarity (M)
formulas.formula: M = (Number of Moles of Solute / Volume of Solution (in litres))
formulas.variables: Number of Moles of Solute, Volume of Solution (in litres)

formulas.name: 2. Molality (m)
formulas.formula: m = (Number of Moles of Solute / Mass of Solvent (in kg))
formulas.variables: Number of Moles of Solute, Mass of Solvent (in kg)
formulas.notes: [for solutions in which the volume of the solvent changes appreciably with temperature]

formulas.name: 3. Mass Percentage
formulas.formula: % w/w = (Mass of Solute / Total Mass of Solution) × 100
formulas.variables: Mass of Solute, Total Mass of Solution

formulas.name: 4. Volume Percentage
formulas.formula: % v/v = (Volume of Solute / Total Volume of Solution) × 100
formulas.variables: Volume of Solute, Total Volume of Solution
formulas.notes: [for solutions in which the solute and solvent are both liquids]

formulas.name: 5. Mole Fraction (X)
formulas.formula: X = (Number of Moles of Solute / Total Number of Moles)
formulas.variables: Number of Moles of Solute, Total Number of Moles

formulas.name: 6. Raoult's Law
formulas.variables: P (partial pressure), P° (vapor pressure of pure solvent), X (mole fraction of solvent)

formulas.name: 7. Henry's Law
formulas.variables: P (partial pressure), k (Henry's Law constant), C (concentration of gas in solution)
